channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dangerous hazard for driving of my drive, 28th June
item.description:
I am writing to report that the trees and vegetation obstructing visibility from my driveway onto the main road have still not been cut back. The overgrown trees make it extremely difficult to see oncoming traffic when pulling out, particularly as the road bends nearby. This presents a serious safety risk, not only for drivers leaving the property but also for pedestrians walking past, who may not be seen by vehicles. This is an ongoing issue that I have had to report several times each year. Last year, I raised concerns about the entire tree needing to be properly trimmed, but I was advised that it was part of a rolling maintenance programme. Unfortunately, the problem has now persisted for over a month again, and the vegetation has once more become a significant hazard. I would appreciate it if this could be treated as a matter of urgency before an accident occurs. I am genuinely concerned that someone could be seriously injured due to the restricted visibility. Please could you arrange for the trees and vegetation to be cut back as soon as possible and confirm when this work is scheduled to take place. Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Station Road, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipped bike, 28th June
item.description:
Fly tipped bike

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 86, Dunstan's Road, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Council are not dealing with road rubbish, 28th June
item.description:
Consistent rubbish is ruining this area and owning a property here. Greenwich council need to up the cleaning to daily in this area and it’s horrible for anyone living here and impossible to sell. Please support tax payers and home owners in the borough.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hawthorne Crescent, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead cat, 28th June
item.description:
Dead cat with open wounds and flies. Distressing to see
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 81, Lawrie Park Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London

item.title: Litter, 28th June
item.description:
The playground, Gunstor Road and Beatty Road absolutely strewn with rubbish

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gunstor Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Franklin road, 28th June
item.description:
Hi, just wanted to report that the entire stretch of Franklin Road, from the Maple Road junction to Croydon Road, is covered in litter and really needs a proper clean. It’s been getting progressively worse and would be great if it could be cleaned up. Thank you!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12, Franklin Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lack of visibility of oncoming traffic - pedestrians crossing the road, 28th June
item.description:
Due to tree growth at base of tree vision of oncoming traffic is obscured and a risk to pedestrians and motorists
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 15, Brantwood Road, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London

item.title: Dead animal, 28th June
item.description:
Appears to be a dead cat in a black sack. The smell is awful and attracting bugs.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Eden Park Avenue, Eden Park, Greater London

item.title: Flytipping daily on Beechwood Rd, 28th June
item.description:
Every single day Please fine them

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kirkland Walk, Hackney, London

item.title: Pavilion smashed open again, 28th June
item.description:
Kids have broken in. Please board up properly
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stanhope Grove, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: SERIOUS HAZARD- Basal growth obstructing view of oncoming traffic for drivers exiting Hammelton Road, 28th June
item.description:
The basal growth around the three trees on London Road is blocking the view of oncoming traffic for drivers exiting Hammelton Road causing a danger to all road users. I have previously reported this to Bromley Council via Fix My Street (FixMyStreet ref: 9544932) who said it was TFLs responsibility, now TFL says they have reported back to Bromley Council. Please confirm who is in charge of the maintenance of these trees. The hazard still remains causing a danger for drivers and pedestrians every day.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): London Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overhanging tree, 28th June
item.description:
Tree overhanging pavement and has been for a while now, impossible for many to walk underneath it so having to walk in cycle lane, needs to be cut back asap before someone gets hit by a cyclist
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hayes Lane, Eden Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large pieces of broken glass on the grass by the pavilion. Lots of litter around the park and pavilion broken into yet again. Grass on sides very overgrown., 28th June
item.description:
Glass and litter and overgrown grass is dangerous to animals and people. Pavilion needs to be closed up with strong metal shutters or demolished.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 107, Stanhope Grove, Upper Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Joints between the concrete slabs have failed creating deep holes., 28th June
item.description:
Path has been in disrepair for years. The joints between the slabs have failed creating potholes. I have witnessed pram wheels and going over. As well as scooters getting caught in the holes and children getting hurt. Especially concerning as this is the path to the playground area. The whole path is like it and needs repair quickly.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tredwell Close, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 28th June
item.description:
Black bags been. Fly tipped adjacent to 18 farmdale road

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Farmdale Road, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Public rubbish bin needed, 28th June
item.description:
Would it be possible to install a rubbish bin? On a daily basis, the street becomes a dumping ground, with litter frequently being left around, particularly at the base of the trees.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woldham Road, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing rubbish bin again, 28th June
item.description:
Bin overflowing. Could I please ask that a longer term solution is found as the day after this is cleaned up rubbish is all over the path again as fixes get in the bin. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Red Post Hill, Dulwich Village, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
